question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. The CPI increased 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis in March 2026, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution. Since 0.3% < 0.8%, the market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3%
Market requires >0.8% increase for YES resolution
Data already released on April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET
No possibility of revision that would change the outcome from 0.3% to >0.8%
risks: Potential data revision by BLS (extremely unlikely to change 0.3% to >0.8%)
Misinterpretation of resolution criteria (unlikely given clear language)
